channel.title: Digital Communication on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Digital Communication on MinWeb

item.title: When Texting Feels Cold: Should You Make the Call?
item.description:
Have you ever stared at your phone, heart rate climbing, trying to figure out if that short, punctuation-free text was playful or just cold? You read it once, then again. You type out a reply, delete it, and start over.

We’ve all been there. Texting is efficient, but it’s a minefield of ambiguity. A 2005 study by Kruger et al. found that people overestimate their ability to convey sarcasm or emotion over text by a staggering 30%. When that “digital silence”—the gap between sent messages—starts to feel like a frost settling over the conversation, the anxiety kicks in.

item.title: Stop Forgetting to Reply: A Simple Trick to Stay Responsive
item.description:
We’ve all been there. Your phone buzzes, you pull it out, and see a text from a friend. You read it, think, “I’ll get to that later,” and then… you completely forget. Hours later, you find the notification, feel a pang of guilt, and realize you’ve officially become “that person”—the one who accidentally ghosts everyone they know.

If you’re drowning in a sea of “sorry for the late reply” messages, you aren’t alone. Honestly, it’s exhausting. In our hyper-connected world, the average person checks their phone 144 times a day. Managing digital communication has become a full-time job that absolutely nobody applied for.
